title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
description: Given the head of a linked list, remove the nth node from the end of the list and return its head.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is sz
thinking_questions: Naive: find length, then traverse to (len-n)th node. Can you do it in one pass?
If two pointers start n+1 apart, what's the relationship between them when the fast pointer reaches the end?
How does a dummy head simplify removing the first node?

hints.L1: Use two pointers n+1 nodes apart. When the fast pointer reaches the end, the slow pointer is right before the node to remove.
hints.L2: Dummy head simplifies edge case of removing head. Advance fast n+1 times. Then advance both until fast is None. slow.next = slow.next.next.
hints.L3:
# INSIGHT: gap of n+1 between pointers → slow lands before target node
dummy = ListNode(0, head)
left = dummy
right = head
for _ in range(n):  # advance right n steps
    right = right.next
while right:  # advance both until right is None
    left = left.next
    right = right.next
left.next = left.next.next  # remove nth from end
return dummy.next
# Time: O(n)  Space: O(1)

follow_up_questions: Why advance fast n steps (not n-1)? Draw it out for a list of 5 nodes, n=2.
Why does the dummy head simplify the case of removing the first node?
What if n were larger than the list length? How would you handle that?
solution_notes: Dummy head + two pointers with gap n. Advance fast n times, then both until fast=None. Remove slow.next. O(n), O(1).
